title: Canonical queue state and append-only queue history are distinct authorities

observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json marks M-001 completed and points to a next continuity item, but it does not enumerate later continuity work.
The append-only queue-update corpus contains a completed M-007 record with dependency on M-006.
A committed discovery gate now scans the queue-update directory and records parse and compatibility failures before reconciliation.
inferred: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is a canonical snapshot, while operations/queue-updates is the authoritative append-only evolution log for work completed after that snapshot.
Selecting continuity work from ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone can repeat completed work or miss the newest handoff.
proposed: Future continuity runs should derive the effective queue from the canonical snapshot plus the validated append-only corpus, then select the newest compatible continuity handoff.
superseded: Treating the newest item visible in ACTIVE_QUEUE.json as necessarily the newest continuity item in repository history.
Treating an externally supplied subset of queue-update records as a complete corpus without directory discovery.
unresolved: The corpus discovery report has not yet been wired into CI against the live repository tree.
No commit-bound effective-queue report currently proves independent agreement between discovered file count and reconciled record count.

contradiction_resolution.apparent_conflict: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json presents M-001 as the continuity endpoint while later commits record M-002 through M-007 work.
contradiction_resolution.resolution: The records describe different temporal layers: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is an older canonical snapshot; append-only queue updates preserve subsequent evolution. Neither record is deleted or rewritten.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch ACTIVE_QUEUE.json at blob abb807e1a25d399f301238f6b8cc26d6be10385f, fetch commits 5355577b8fa40cc2c822b4ec2078d8845cf6ae80 and 9a60c0a791cab631b7f36e63afd2b72749b1e75e, and reject this memory if M-007 is not a later continuity record or if the corpus gate does not discover queue-update JSON files before compatibility evaluation.
next_handoff: Integrate buildQueueUpdateCorpusReport into a commit-bound effective-queue report and require reconciliation_allowed plus independent discovered-file-count agreement before selecting the next continuity item.
